question.content: For an infinitely long straight wire carrying current I along the z-axis, write the magnetic-field vector B at a point whose cylindrical radial distance from the wire is r. Use mu_0 for the vacuum permeability and keep the azimuthal unit-vector notation in ASCII form, such as hat(theta). Answer format: a single algebraic expression. Preserve the ASCII notation style used in the problem, including forms such as hat(theta); do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Construct the pseudo-3D cylindrical geometry around a straight z-axis wire with radial distance r.
step_1.key_point: Magnetic field is azimuthal by symmetry.

step_3.content: Use Ampere loop around the wire so B is tangential and constant on the circle.

step_5.content: Map B direction to thetahat and then set curl(A)=B using only Az(r) by axisymmetry.

step_7.content: Integrate with respect to r to obtain logarithmic Az and choose reference constant by gauge.
